what_it_does: Defines a CanvasHeader JSX component that renders a canvas header with a title, optional subtitle, optional action area, and assigns the component to window.CanvasHeader.
observations: CanvasHeader accepts title, sub, and actions through destructured props.
The title is always rendered in an h1 with class dsa-canvas-title.
The subtitle paragraph is rendered only when sub is truthy.
The actions container is rendered only when actions is truthy.
The component is exposed by assigning it to window.CanvasHeader.
standouts: The component is a small presentational header wrapper.
It uses conditional JSX for optional subtitle and actions regions.
It relies on a browser global assignment for exposure.
conventions: Uses PascalCase for the component name.
Uses dsa-prefixed CSS class names for structural styling hooks.
Uses JSX truthiness checks for optional regions.
Does not show an ES module export in this file.
concerns: Relies on window being available when the file is evaluated.
No prop validation or default values are shown in this file.
notes: A compact shared canvas header component with fixed title structure and optional subtitle/actions slots.
questions: Is window.CanvasHeader the intended component registration mechanism for the surrounding runtime?
What shape of content is expected for actions?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it provides a shared canvas header component.
intention_signals: It seems intended to standardize canvas header markup while allowing screens to supply subtitle text and action controls.
novelty: The explicit window.CanvasHeader assignment is the distinct exposure pattern in this file.
contribution: Provides a reusable canvas header structure with a consistent title block, optional subtitle, optional actions region, and named CSS hooks that a fused system would keep as a header substrate.
summary_for_embedding: This file defines a CanvasHeader JSX component that renders a title, optional subtitle, optional actions area, and registers the component on window.CanvasHeader.
intention_for_embedding: This was for sharing a consistent canvas header layout across views while leaving subtitle and action content configurable.

symbols.does: Renders a canvas header with a required title and optional subtitle and actions areas.
symbols.description: CanvasHeader is a functional JSX component that destructures title, sub, and actions from its props. It always renders the title inside a named title block, conditionally renders sub as paragraph text, and conditionally renders actions inside a separate actions container.
